metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4818
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3: confocal immunofluorescence imagings showed auditory inner hair cells (IHC) lack Munc13 and CAPS priming factors.

Figure 4a: co-staining of Otoferlin (OTOF) and CtBP2 showed Otoferlin is enriched in IHC ribbon synapse active zones.

Figure 4b-g: electron tomography of IHC synapses showed vesicular tethers at active zones observed in wildtype condition were abolished in otoferlin-deficient IHCs. Otoferlin appears to be required to form short tethers (<10nm) between vesicles and active zone membranes.

It remains unclear whether Otoferlin is a tether constituent and, if so, homophilic interactions of Otoferlin molecules localized to vesicular and plasma membranes are involved.. Experimental description: M&M: "otoferlin-KO (Otof-KO; Reisinger et al., 2011) mice of either sex were used to prepare organotypic cultures of the organ of Corti as described previously (Nouvian et al., 2011; Reisinger et al., 2011)."

antibody:
mouse or rabbit anti-otoferlin antibodies (Abcam/Synaptic Systems)

checks.label: OWL Consistency
